member.name: Olly Glover
member.synopsis: Olly Glover is the Liberal Democrat MP for Didcot and Wantage , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024. He currently undertakes the role of Liberal Democrat Spokesperson (Transport).

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Delivery Services: Robotics
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what powers local authorities have to restrict or prohibit the use of pavement delivery robots within their areas.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Where pavement robots currently operate, they do so in partnership with the relevant local authority.The Government announced last week that it will facilitate new trials of pavement delivery robots in new places through powers in the Regulating for Growt...

writtenQuestions.heading: Veterans: Disability Aids
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what assessment he has made of the impact on veterans of the absence of long-term funding arrangements for the Veterans Mobility Fund and Veterans Hearing Loss Fund.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the hon. Member to the answer I gave on 1 July 2026 to Questions 12265 and 12266 to the hon. Member for Mid Buckinghamshire (Mr Smith).https://questions-statements.parliament.uk/written-questions/detail/2026-06-23/12265

writtenQuestions.heading: Veterans: Disability Aids
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what discussions his department has had regarding the future of the Veterans Mobility Fund beyond March 2027.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the hon. Member to the answer I gave on 1 July 2026 to Questions 12265 and 12266 to the hon. Member for Mid Buckinghamshire (Mr Smith).https://questions-statements.parliament.uk/written-questions/detail/2026-06-23/12265

writtenQuestions.heading: Criminal Injuries Compensation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, what steps his Department is taking to ensure that the Criminal Injuries Compensation Authority processes and decides applications for compensation in a timely manner.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I understand that applicants have endured traumatic experiences. Compensation is a way in which we recognise victims’ suffering. Last year we paid over £164m in compensation to victims seriously injured or bereaved by violent crime and two thirds of new a...

writtenQuestions.heading: Supported Housing: Older People

writtenQuestions.answerText: My Department has made no estimate of the number of unoccupied retirement flats across the country. The government does not provide dedicated funding for the construction of new retirement housing. Grant funding from the Social and Affordable Homes Progra...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Post Offices: Business Rates
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, whether they have any plans to consider business rates relief for post offices.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the hon. Member to the answers given to PQ UIN 125750 and UIN 128242.

writtenQuestions.heading: Post Offices: Business Rates
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, whether the Government has considering undertaking an impact assessment of the impact of business rates on post offices.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the hon. Member to the answers given to PQ UIN 125750 and UIN 128242.

writtenQuestions.heading: Railways: Nationalisation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what assessment she has made of the total expected cost saving and revenue generation opportunities from bringing train operating companies into state ownership.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Public ownership is expected to generate significant savings and revenue generation opportunities for Government. For example, once all currently franchised services have transferred, public ownership will save the taxpayer up to £110 million to £150 mill...

writtenQuestions.heading: Great British Railways
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what assessment she has made of the total expected cost saving and revenue generation opportunities from the reorganisation of the railway industry into Great British Railways.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The creation of Great British Railways (GBR) will reduce fragmentation and improve whole-system decision making in the rail industry. Integration of track and train is expected to lead to significant cost efficiencies. A large share of these efficiencies ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Space: Research
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what steps she is taking to retain specialist staff in the space research and development sector.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK Space Agency, as part of DSIT, supports the retention of specialist staff in the space research and development sector through programme funding and enabling long-term international collaborations, including participation in ESA programmes and with...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Space: Research
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what assessment her Department has made of the impact of previous research grant cuts on UK space research and development.

writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Active Travel: Women
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what information her Department holds on whether women and girls are more likely to experience abuse and harassment while (a) walking and (b) cycling, compared to using public transport.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The ‘Personal Safety on Transport’ survey research was published in February this year. The findings are based on a survey of 4,900 adults across Great Britain and show the proportion of public transport users who experienced things that undermine persona...

writtenQuestions.heading: Speed Limits: Rural Areas
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what assessment she has made of the impact of the 60mph default speed limit on rural roads on safety; and whether she plans to review this default.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department for Transport keeps speed limit policy under review as part of its wider road safety work. As set out in the Road Safety Strategy published on 7 January 2026, the Department is updating the Setting Local Speed Limits guidance, to support au...

writtenQuestions.heading: Sheltered Housing: Service Charges
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what assessment his Department has made of the potential impact of high service charges and low resale prices of inherited retirement flats on people living in those flats.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the hon. Member to the answer given to Question UIN 4726 on 8 June 2026.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Pre-school Education: Special Educational Needs
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what additional support her Department is providing to teachers in response to the increased number of pupils with special educational needs and disabilities in early years settings.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department recognises the increasing demand for support for children with special educational needs and disabilities (SEND) in early years settings. We are investing in workforce development through our Best Start in Life strategy and wider SEND refor...

writtenQuestions.heading: Social Rented Housing: Pets
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, with reference to the Renters Rights Act 2025, whether he is taking steps to extend the right to request permission to keep a pet to people living in social housing.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government recognises that pets can bring joy and comfort to their owners, as well as supporting their mental and physical wellbeing.On 12 February 2026, Baroness Taylor wrote to all social housing landlords setting out my Department’s position on fai...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government
